commit.message:
fix(terrain): use player position for chunk LOD, not camera position

Third-person camera is ~15m behind the player. Using camera position meant
the chunk under the player had dist=1 (128 segs) while the chunk behind
the camera had dist=0 (256 segs) - causing visible LOD seam under feet.

- WorldManager.update() now accepts playerX/playerZ parameters
- Falls back to camera position if player not available
- Aethelgard.update() passes player entity position to worldManager.update()

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7551)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Enforce formatter/linter boundary: remove GML-domain comment constants from normalizeFormattedOutput

The formatter's post-processing pipeline must not embed knowledge of
specific GML-domain comment strings synthesized by lint rules. The
removed `ensureBlankLineBetweenVertexFormatComments` function stored two
GML-API-specific explanatory comment constants and inserted blank lines
between them when they appeared adjacent in formatted output.

This violates target-state.md §2.1 and §3.2: the formatter is layout-only
and must not make spacing decisions based on the semantic content of
lint-generated comment text. Spacing decisions rooted in GML API semantics
belong exclusively in @gml-modules/lint.

The removed function was also dead code: KEEP_VERTEX_FORMAT_COMMENT_TEXT
read "completed within a function call" but the lint gm2012 fixture
generates "built within a function call", so the pattern never matched.

Adds a boundary-enforcement test to normalize-output-formatter-boundary.test.ts
documenting the enforced contract with accurate lint fixture strings.

Fix shape property evaluation to use composition frame (fixes rocket flame TrimPath at frame 28) (#57)

* Initial plan

* Initial progress tracking

Co-authored-by: cg-zhou <9943470+cg-zhou@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: evaluate shape properties at composition frame to match lottie-web

lottie-web uses `frameNum = comp.renderedFrame - offsetTime` compared
against `kf.t - offsetTime`, which is mathematically equivalent to
evaluating at `comp.renderedFrame` vs raw `kf.t` values. Our code was
evaluating at `layer_frame = comp_frame - st` against raw `kf.t`, which
is wrong when `st != 0`.

Add `current_comp_frame` field to Player. In render_layer, set it to
`comp_frame` (before applying start_time offset). All shape property
evaluations (TrimPath, Fill, Stroke, GradientFill, GradientStroke,
Rect, Ellipse, Path, Polystar, Repeater, group Transform, Rounding,
Text, and masks) now use `current_comp_frame`.

This fixes the rocket flame at frame 28: TrimPath keyframes at t=21/28
for `e` and t=23/30 for `s` (all in composition time) are now correctly
evaluated, producing a visible flame segment matching lottie-web.

Co-authored-by: cg-zhou <9943470+cg-zhou@users.noreply.github.com>

* Resolve merge conflicts: incorporate main's fill-rule and MergePaths changes

Pull in upstream changes from main (#56):
- FillShape/GradientFillShape gain fill_rule field (from shape r property)
- Player sets fill-rule per-fill-shape rather than from MergePaths mode
- MergePaths is now a no-op (matching lottie-web behavior)
- parser, model, and test files updated from main

Our current_comp_frame changes are preserved on top of these changes.

Co-authored-by: cg-zhou <9943470+cg-zhou@users.noreply.github.com>

- Codex: `-c model_reasoning_effort="<mapped>"` (max → xhigh)
- Cursor: model variant resolution (appends `-thinking` for high/max)
- OpenCode: `--variant <level>` (max → high)
- Copilot/Gemini/VSCode/Antigravity: graceful no-op

Resolution chain: CLI --effort → ai.<command>.effort → ai.effort → None

Changes:
- Add EffortLevel enum (low/medium/high/max) and supports_effort capability
- Add effort fields to AICommandConfig, AIConfig, and ProjectConfig
- Add effort_args() and resolve_effort_model() to AbstractAITool base
- Implement effort in Claude, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode adapters
- Add resolve_effort() service with full fallback chain
- Update confirm_ai_selection() to 3-tuple (tool, model, effort)
- Add --effort to plan-task, implement-task, batch, and deps commands
- Add effort prompt to `wade init` flow
- Add 57 unit tests covering all effort functionality

Closes #38

stability: add docstrings, frontend static serving, and K8s API timeouts

- Add complete module/class/function docstrings to all Python files in
  backend/ and ai-engine/ (main, database, utils, models, services,
  controllers, repositories, router, analyzers)

- Backend now conditionally serves the React SPA from frontend/build/
  when the directory is present (K8s single-pod / local dev mode):
  * Mounts frontend/build/static at /static via StaticFiles
  * Adds /{full_path:path} catch-all that returns index.html for any
    non-API, non-static route, enabling client-side React Router
  * Root GET / keeps its JSON health-check response for backwards compat
  * FRONTEND_BUILD_DIR env var overrides the default build path

- Add explicit _request_timeout parameters to all Kubernetes Python
  client calls in pod_service.py and the cluster-status endpoint:
  * List calls: 30 s  (pod listing, event listing)
  * Read calls: 15 s  (single pod object)
  * Log calls:  20 s  (pod log streaming)
  * Cluster status probe: 10 s
  Prevents indefinite hangs when the K8s API server is unreachable or
  slow inside the cluster

All 28 existing tests continue to pass.

commit.message:
fix(deps): bump `slotmap` minimum version to `1.1.0` (#2640)

`slotmap` introduced `const fn from_ffi` in `1.1.0`, but all three
crates were pinned to `1.0.0`, causing compilation failures when that
API is used.

## Changes
- Bumped `slotmap` from `1.0.0` → `1.1.0` in:
  - `dfir_rs/Cargo.toml`
  - `dfir_lang/Cargo.toml`
  - `hydro_lang/Cargo.toml`

The resolved version in `Cargo.lock` is already `1.1.1`, so no lock file
churn is expected.

---------
